title: Daily Briefing — Apr 08
headline: Extreme fear meets rotational green shoots — flow says sit tight, not run.
body: Right, so VIX is elevated at 25.87 and Fear & Greed is flashing 22.5 — that's Extreme Fear, folks. Breadth has been rubbish, but price action shows SPY holding just above its SMA20 (659.22 vs 656.65) and tech names like NVDA and AAPL clinging to their mean reversion zones. No major daily close cracks on the 20 MA, so we stay patient.

Sector rotation's brewing: Energy (XLE 60.16), Utilities (XLU 46.27), Real Estate (XLRE 41.72) — all leading and showing 'LOW' risk. Meanwhile, Discretionary (XLY), Healthcare (XLV), and Staples (XLP) are lagging or weakening. This is classic defensive rotation — money moving away from risk and into yield and shelter plays.

Contrarian lens: Extreme Fear is actually an opportunity per my rulebook (R013). We won’t chase bloody prints — abundance mindset. Watch for smart money footprints around XLK/XLU; elevated VIX plus improving tech/utility flows is often the setup for mean reversion bounce. No major bond breakdowns (HYG/LQD intact), so the 'bonds freak out, everything breaks' trigger hasn’t fired. If it's in the press, it's already priced — don’t get caught in the panic.
callout_body:
SPY: 656.65 (SMA20 support), 673.84 (SMA50 resistance)
NVDA: 177.39 (SMA20), 182.31 (SMA50)
XLU: 45.92 (SMA20, leading)
XLE: 59.22 (SMA20)
VIX: 25.87 (Elevated — watch for spike reversals)
Fear & Greed: 22.5 (Extreme Fear, be opportunistic)
No active breakdowns on HYG/LQD (macro greenlight)
action_items: If SPY or XLK closes below daily SMA20, exit risk — otherwise, lean into mean reversion setups on leading sectors (XLU, XLE, XLRE).
Set alerts for NVDA and AAPL daily closes around SMA20 — accumulation possible if flows show up with Extreme Fear backdrop.
closing_quote: If everyone's scared, I'm shopping — follow the flows, not the headlines.
